items.text: Civil Protection Claim / Domestic Restraining Order

children.label: Civil Protection Claim / Domestic Restraining Order

candidates.label: Criminal Law
candidates.definition: Law concerned with the punishment of individuals who commit crimes.

candidates.label: Defendant
candidates.definition: A defendant or respondent is a person or group against whom a criminal or civil action is brought. Actions include dispute matters (litigation, arbitration or mediation) or bankruptcy matters.

candidates.label: Public Defender
candidates.definition: A public defender is a lawyer appointed by the court to represent individuals who cannot afford to hire their own legal counsel.

candidates.label: Criminal Record
candidates.definition: A criminal record is an official compilation of an individual's past arrests, charges, convictions, and other interactions with the criminal justice system. This record can have implications for employment, housing, and other opportunities, as many entities conduct background checks. In some jurisdictions, certain convictions can be expunged or sealed, removing them from public view.

candidates.label: Criminal Citation
candidates.definition: In the context of criminal law, a citation is a formal notice or document issued by law enforcement officers to an individual, alleging a minor violation or offense, such as a traffic infraction. Rather than arresting the individual, the citation instructs them to appear in court on a specified date to address the violation, or in some cases, to pay a fine or take corrective action. Failure to comply with the terms of a citation can lead to additional penalties or warrants for arrest.

candidates.label: Criminal Proceedings
candidates.definition: Criminal Proceedings refer to the legal process of prosecuting and punishing individuals who have been accused of committing a crime, including all related proceedings such as indictment, grand jury, sentencing, and parole.

candidates.label: Motor Vehicle Law
candidates.definition: Law covering all the aspects of registering and titling motor vehicles, and licensing drivers.

candidates.label: Criminal Law
candidates.definition: Law concerned with the punishment of individuals who commit crimes.

candidates.label: Driver
candidates.definition: A driver is an individual who operates a motor vehicle, controlling its direction and speed on public or private roads. This role requires adherence to traffic laws and regulations to ensure safety and order on thoroughfares.

candidates.label: Speeding Ticket
candidates.definition: A speeding ticket is a written notice issued by a law enforcement officer to a driver who is alleged to have exceeded the posted speed limit. The ticket serves as a formal accusation of the speeding violation and typically includes information about the offense, the fine amount, and the steps the driver must take to resolve the matter.

candidates.label: Driving Under the Influence
candidates.definition: Driving or operating a motor vehicle or common carrier while mentally or physically impaired as the result of consuming an alcoholic beverage or using a drug or narcotic.
synonyms: Conducir bajo los efectos del alcohol
Driving Under the Influence of Intoxicants
Dirigir sob influência de álcool ou drogas
נהיגה תחת השפעת סמים או אלכוהול
मादक पदार्थों के अधीन गाड़ी चलाना

candidates.label: Boating DUI/BUI
candidates.definition: Operating a boat or watercraft while under the influence of drugs or alcohol, which is classified as a criminal misdemeanor offense.
synonyms: Conduite en état d'ivresse/drogue en bateau
Dirigir embarcação sob efeito de álcool/drogas
ボートでの飲酒運転/薬物運転

candidates.label: Substance Abuse and Drug Testing Law
candidates.definition: Drug Testing & Substance Abuse Law refers to the legal framework governing the testing of individuals for the presence of drugs and the regulation of substance abuse. It includes policies, procedures, and regulations related to the detection, prevention, and treatment of drug use and addiction.

hierarchy_path.label: Substance Abuse and Drug Testing Law

candidates.label: Criminal Law
candidates.definition: Law concerned with the punishment of individuals who commit crimes.

candidates.label: Drug Test
candidates.definition: A drug test is a technical analysis of a biological specimen, such as urine, hair, blood, breath, sweat, or oral fluid/saliva, to determine the presence or absence of specified drugs or their metabolites. These tests are often used by employers, sports organizations, legal and law enforcement agencies, and in healthcare settings.

candidates.label: Criminal Proceedings
candidates.definition: Criminal Proceedings refer to the legal process of prosecuting and punishing individuals who have been accused of committing a crime, including all related proceedings such as indictment, grand jury, sentencing, and parole.

candidates.label: Cannabis Law
candidates.definition: Cannabis Law refers to the legal rules and regulations governing the use, possession, sale, cultivation, and distribution of cannabis, both for medical and recreational purposes. These laws vary significantly between jurisdictions, ranging from total prohibition to decriminalization or full legalization, and they may involve aspects of criminal, business, and health law among others

candidates.label: Criminal Law
candidates.definition: Law concerned with the punishment of individuals who commit crimes.

candidates.label: Substance Abuse and Drug Testing Law
candidates.definition: Drug Testing & Substance Abuse Law refers to the legal framework governing the testing of individuals for the presence of drugs and the regulation of substance abuse. It includes policies, procedures, and regulations related to the detection, prevention, and treatment of drug use and addiction.

hierarchy_path.label: Substance Abuse and Drug Testing Law

candidates.label: Substance Abuse Policy
candidates.definition: A substance abuse policy is a formal document established by an organization to outline its stance on the use, possession, and distribution of drugs and alcohol in the workplace. It aims to ensure a safe and productive work environment by defining prohibited behaviors, setting forth procedures for testing and handling violations, and providing resources for prevention and treatment.

candidates.label: Drug Test
candidates.definition: A drug test is a technical analysis of a biological specimen, such as urine, hair, blood, breath, sweat, or oral fluid/saliva, to determine the presence or absence of specified drugs or their metabolites. These tests are often used by employers, sports organizations, legal and law enforcement agencies, and in healthcare settings.

candidates.label: Criminal Proceedings
candidates.definition: Criminal Proceedings refer to the legal process of prosecuting and punishing individuals who have been accused of committing a crime, including all related proceedings such as indictment, grand jury, sentencing, and parole.

candidates.label: Drug Crimes
candidates.definition: The violation of laws prohibiting the production, distribution, and/or use of certain controlled substances and the equipment or devices utilized in their preparation and/or use.

candidates.label: Criminal Record
candidates.definition: A criminal record is an official compilation of an individual's past arrests, charges, convictions, and other interactions with the criminal justice system. This record can have implications for employment, housing, and other opportunities, as many entities conduct background checks. In some jurisdictions, certain convictions can be expunged or sealed, removing them from public view.

candidates.label: Criminal Proceedings
candidates.definition: Criminal Proceedings refer to the legal process of prosecuting and punishing individuals who have been accused of committing a crime, including all related proceedings such as indictment, grand jury, sentencing, and parole.

candidates.label: Criminal Court
candidates.definition: A Criminal Court is a specific type of court that has jurisdiction over criminal cases and is responsible for administering proceedings related to criminal law violations.

candidates.label: Assault Law
candidates.definition: Law relating to inflicting physical harm or unwanted physical contact upon a person or a threat or attempt to commit such an action.

candidates.label: Rape
candidates.definition: The carnal knowledge of a person, forcibly and/or against that person’s will or not forcibly or against the person’s will in instances where the victim is incapable of giving consent because of his/her temporary or permanent mental or physical incapacity.

candidates.label: Domestic Violence
candidates.definition: Domestic Violence refers to physical, emotional, or sexual abuse that occurs within a domestic relationship, such as between spouses or partners, and is considered a form of criminal assault.
synonyms: ドメスティックバイオレンス

items.item_text: Civil Protection Claim / Domestic Restraining Order

candidates.label: Civil Protection Claim / Domestic Restraining Order
candidates.definition: A Civil Protection Claim / Domestic Restraining Order is a legal order issued by a court that prohibits an individual from contacting or coming near another person, typically a family member, in order to protect them from harm or abuse.
synonyms: Civil Protection Claim / Domestic Restraining Order
Demanda de protección civil / Orden de restricción doméstica
Orden de protección civil / Orden de alejamiento doméstico
Reivindicação de proteção civil / Ordem de restrição doméstica
Réclamation de protection civile / Ordonnance de restriction domestique
תביעת הגנת אזרחים / צו הגבלת מגורים במקום
सिविल संरक्षा दावा / घरेलू रोकथाम आदेश
市民防護請求 / 家庭内制限命令
民事保护索赔 / 国内限制令

hierarchy_path.label: Civil Protection Claim / Domestic Restraining Order

candidates.label: Personal Property Law
candidates.definition: Matters relating to personal property — not real property — such as damage to vehicles, ownership of valuables (e.g., jewelry), trespass to chattels, etc.

candidates.label: Criminal Matter Identifiers
candidates.definition: Criminal Matter Identifiers are unique codes or numbers used to track and identify criminal cases, including information such as law enforcement arrest tracking numbers, fatality indicators, commercial motor vehicle indicators, law enforcement incident tracking numbers, and traffic accident indicators.

candidates.label: Burglary
candidates.definition: The unlawful entry into a building or other structure with the intent to commit a felony or a theft.
synonyms: Robo con fuerza en las cosas

candidates.label: Shoplifting
candidates.definition: The act of taking merchandise from a store without paying for it, which is classified as a criminal offense of a minor nature.

candidates.label: Business and Financial Crimes Law
candidates.definition: Fraud, embezzlement, ponzi, etc. False Claims Act.
synonyms: Lei de Crimes Empresariais e Financeiros
Ley de Delitos Empresariales y Financieros
Loi sur les crimes économiques et financiers

candidates.label: Juvenile Detention Center
candidates.definition: A Juvenile Detention Center is a secure facility designed to detain minors who are awaiting court hearings or have been adjudicated for juvenile offenses. These centers focus on rehabilitation and education, providing programs tailored to the developmental needs of young offenders.

candidates.label: Financial Statement
candidates.definition: A Financial Statement is a formal record of the financial activities and position of a business, organization, or individual, typically presented in structured manner for a specific time period. Common types of financial statements include income statements, balance sheets, and cash flow statements.

candidates.label: Juvenile Law
candidates.definition: Juvenile law and child protective proceedings.
synonyms: Direito da Infância e da Juventude

candidates.label: Wire Fraud
candidates.definition: The use of an electric or electronic communications facility to intentionally transmit a false and/or deceptive message in furtherance of a fraudulent activity.

candidates.label: Fraud
candidates.definition: Fraud is the intentional act of deceiving someone in order to gain an unfair advantage or cause harm.

candidates.label: Fraud and Economic Torts Law
candidates.definition: The wrong done by another person or business that occurs in, or affects, commerce.
synonyms: Droit de la fraude et des délits économiques
Lei de Fraude e Delitos Econômicos
Ley de Fraude y Agravios Económicos
Ley de Fraude y Delitos Económicos
詐欺および経済侵害法
